In the present case, the stipulated amount under the ATS was in 
the nature of an earnest money deposit and thus, Section 74 of the 
1872 Act cannot apply to the same. Further, the forfeiture clause was 
fair and equitable rather than one-sided and unconscionable, as it 
imposed liabilities on both the appellant-purchaser and respondent-
sellers, wherein the seller was obligated to pay twice the advance 
amount paid by the buyer in case of his default. 
52. Even, for argument’s sake, if we have to apply the principle under 
Section 74 of the 1872 Act to the present case in line with Kailash 
Nath (supra), the forfeiture of the entire amount of advance money 
by the respondent nos. 1-4 would still be justified on the ground that
